title: How to avoid headaches with(in) treepac
text: treepac, beyond fulfilling requirements class project, aims streamline frequent function calls exploratory data analyses. Part motivation behind package’s functions came exercises dealing economic time series datasets. Percentage changes elasticities often utilized takes lines R actually calculate. treepac’s pctchg llreg aim address problem. Lastly, theme_3pac good add-ggplot objects improve visual clarity.

title: How to avoid headaches with(in) treepac
text: following note applies two functions: llreg pctchg functions utilize base R dataframe slicing selection (instead Tidyverse solutions), requires user pass target column names quotes (’’ “” fine). hand, dataframes’ names passed quotes. Additional information (parts note) can accessed function’s help page.

title: A 3-Pack of Neat Functions
text: function supposed show elasticity 2 variables (words, linear relationship natural log y natural log x):
code: # the function takes 3 arguments # treedat is the package's dataset # remember to pass the column names in quotes! llreg(treedat, "x", "y") #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = log(df[[y]]) ~ log(df[[x]])) #>  #> Residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -1.23697 -0.28854  0.07056  0.29880  1.07239  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)   1.54410    0.09353   16.51   <2e-16 *** #> log(df[[x]])  1.96438    0.05335   36.82   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.4644 on 98 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.9326, Adjusted R-squared:  0.9319  #> F-statistic:  1356 on 1 and 98 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

title: A 3-Pack of Neat Functions
text: function takes 2 arguments, dataframe, numeric column within returns additional column shows entry--entry percentage changes target column:

title: A 3-Pack of Neat Functions
text: ggplot2 theme based theme_bw(). Atop theme, changes made improve visual clarity legibility:
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(data = treedat, aes(x = x, y = y, col = z)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +   labs(title = 'Example scatter with spline fitting',        subtitle = 'theme_3pac() example',        caption = 'Data were randomly generated and are not reflective of any real-life trend') +   theme_3pac() #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Log-Log Regression — llreg
text: quick way estimate elasticity 2 variables.

title: Log-Log Regression — llreg
text: df data frame containing variables interest x predictor / explanatory variable, passed quotes y response variable, passed quotes

title: Log-Log Regression — llreg
text: elasticity, intercept, standard error, p-value estimates regression output

title: Log-Log Regression — llreg
code: llreg(mtcars, 'mpg', 'cyl') #>  #> Call: #> lm(formula = log(df[[y]]) ~ log(df[[x]])) #>  #> Residuals: #>      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max  #> -0.29826 -0.09360  0.03607  0.09594  0.29890  #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)     #> (Intercept)   4.39519    0.29439  14.930 1.99e-15 *** #> log(df[[x]]) -0.88485    0.09905  -8.933 5.92e-10 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Residual standard error: 0.1642 on 30 degrees of freedom #> Multiple R-squared:  0.7268,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.7177  #> F-statistic:  79.8 on 1 and 30 DF,  p-value: 5.916e-10 #>

title: Calculating Percentage Changes — pctchg
text: pctchg takes dataframe numeric column calculate percentage changes one period next. values missing/0, function return 0 observation "pctchg_" column. Time series dataframes recommended.

title: Calculating Percentage Changes — pctchg
text: df dataframe containing column interest col name column, passed quotes

title: Calculating Percentage Changes — pctchg
text: new dataframe additional column containing "pctchg_"

title: A Clean ggplot2 Theme — theme_3pac
text: theme_3pac function based theme_bw, meant improve visual clarity ggplot objects.

title: A Clean ggplot2 Theme — theme_3pac
text: base_size numerical value, size plot's fonts, default value 12 base_family family fonts graph based , default value 'sans'

title: A Clean ggplot2 Theme — theme_3pac
text: additional function within ggplot() call

title: A Clean ggplot2 Theme — theme_3pac
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(data = treedat, aes(x = x, y = y, col = z)) +   geom_point() +   geom_smooth(se = FALSE) +   labs(title = 'Example scatter with best fit',        subtitle = 'theme_3pac() example',        caption = 'Data were randomly generated and are not reflective of any real-life trend') +   theme_3pac() #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'
